title: linaria reflexa subsp. doumetii
intro: Linaria reflexa subsp. doumetii, also known as the toadflax or wild snapdragon, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Linaria reflexa subsp. doumetii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
